name: Saving the Chesapeake: Fifty Years of Bay Restoration
description: Documentary on the decades-long effort to restore water quality, oysters, and grasses in the Chesapeake Bay. Shared by the Annapolis Library for its environmental film series.

name: The Tree Army: The Civilian Conservation Corps
description: History film on the CCC and the young men who built parks, planted forests, and shaped America's public lands during the Depression. Part of the library's American history screening series.

name: America's Best Idea: A History of the National Parks
description: Feature documentary tracing how the national park system was created and the people who fought to protect these landscapes. Shared for the library's environmental and history series.

name: Annapolis Through the Centuries: A Local History Talk
description: Recorded lecture from the Annapolis Library's local-history series on the city's colonial founding, the State House, and life along the Severn River.

name: First Strokes: A Watercolor Demonstration
description: Step-by-step watercolor demonstration recorded for the library's art-instruction series, covering washes, wet-on-wet blending, and painting a simple marsh landscape.

name: Blue Note: A History of American Jazz
description: Music documentary on the roots and evolution of jazz, from New Orleans through bebop and beyond. Shared by the library for its jazz-history film night.

name: The Great Marsh: Wetlands of the Atlantic Flyway
description: Nature documentary on the tidal marshes and migratory birds of the Chesapeake and the Atlantic Flyway. Shared for the library's environmental screening series.

name: Rivers Reborn: Restoring America's Watersheds
description: Environmental documentary on dam removals and watershed restoration projects reviving rivers and native fish runs across the country. Part of the library film series.

name: The Land We Share: A History of American Conservation
description: Lecture-format documentary on the rise of the conservation movement and the policy debates over public land and resource management. Shared for the library's history and policy series.
